title: grumilea keilii
intro: Grumilea keilii, a species of flowering plant in the family Lamiaceae, is a perennial herb native to Australia. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine, with reported medicinal properties including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial activities. Grumilea keilii has been associated with various health benefits, including relief from pain, inflammation, and digestive issues, although more research is needed to confirm its efficacy. Herbal preparations of Grumilea keilii are typically made from the leaves and stems, and can be consumed as teas, infusions, or extracts, although proper dosing and preparation methods should be carefully followed.
